contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 40
- Description: Partner Poke Scheduler v0.1 — auto-file Partner Check-in on send
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/130
  
  Details:
  Closed the manual gap between 'draft sent in Gmail' and 'Partner Check-ins tab updated.' Without this v0.1, LLM advisors and the bell would keep recommending pokes for partners Gary had just emailed because the operator hadn't yet manually filed the check-in.
  
  ## What shipped
  - PartnerPokeDrafts.gs now has runProcessSentPartnerPokes() — new public function clasp-pushed to the live Apps Script project (14gKJ0VW49RsSn4S03pgxKXy0sp4Z7Z3Wm1Wj8jQiWW5dj1sFuPnp95sh).
  - Menu item 'Process sent pokes (auto-checkin)' added to the spreadsheet menu.
  - agentic_ai_context PR #130 (merged) — full v0.1 architecture, design choices, idempotency convention, status enum, and operator setup instructions captured in PARTNER_POKE_SCHEDULER_v0.md.
  
  ## Architecture
  Scans Partner Poke Drafts audit log for rows with status='draft_created':
  - Draft still exists in Gmail → skip (try again next run).
  - Draft gone + matching Sent message (in:sent label:AI/Partner Poke to:<email> newer_than:14d, exact subject match):
    - If is_self_poke=true → mark log row 'sent_self_poke' (no check-in filed; actual partner contact hasn't happened).
    - Else → append Partner Check-in row (Method=Email, Stock Status=Unknown, Next Check-in Date BLANK, Update ID=poke-auto-<draft_id>, Submitted By='Partner Poke Scheduler v0.1'); mark log 'sent_and_filed'.
  - Draft gone + no matching Sent message → mark log 'discarded' with note.
  
  ## Key design choices
  - Idempotency via Update ID = poke-auto-<draft_id> on Partner Check-ins col K (same dedup convention as the human-driven scanner). Re-running the scanner over the same audit row is a no-op once the check-in is filed.
  - Next Check-in Date stays BLANK by design — operator-driven cadence stays in operator's hands; auto-row records what happened, not what the operator intends. Operator files a follow-up entry manually when there's a reply or a chosen next-check-in date.
  - Self-poke drafts get a different status ('sent_self_poke') without a check-in entry — because sending the reminder to yourself doesn't constitute partner contact. The check-in is filed when Gary later acts on the offline channel.
  
  ## What v0.1 still doesn't do (queued for v0.2)
  - Detect partner replies and either auto-file a Method=Email follow-up check-in (with Stock Status inferred by Grok) or just notify the operator. Defer until calibration week reveals what reply patterns look like.
  - Auto-set Next Check-in Date. Intentional — the operator-driven cadence is the whole point.
  
  PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/130 (merged)
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 66.67
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
